verdict_reasoning: Calreticulin is primarily an ER-resident chaperone, but it translocates to the outer plasma membrane during immunogenic cell death (ICD) and apoptosis as a canonical 'eat me' signal (DAMP). Surface-exposed calreticulin is recognized by C1q and integrins on immune cells. This surface pool is stress/cell-state-induced rather than basal, qualifying as contextual via induced cell-death ecto-translocation.
